mission.title: vitess: RFC: Atomic Distributed Transactions
mission.description: RFC: Atomic Distributed Transactions. Requested by 5+ users.

steps.title: Check current vitess deployment
steps.description:
Verify your vitess version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n vitess -l app.kubernetes.io/name=vitess
helm list -n vitess 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working vitess installation.

steps.title: Review vitess configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant vitess configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n vitess -l app.kubernetes.io/name=vitess
kubectl get configmap -n vitess -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=vitess
```
## Existing System Overview
Vitess has three transaction modes; those are Single, Multi and TwoPC.

In Single Mode, any transaction that spans more than one shard is rolled back immediately.

steps.title: Apply the fix for RFC: Atomic Distributed Transactions
steps.description:
This PR reject distributed transaction prepare on reserved connection. These are the connection that are using temporary tables or locking feature. They are not safe to allow support for atomic commit.
```yaml
Case 5: When Create Transaction Record fails. TM will roll back the transaction.
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n vitess -l app.kubernetes.io/name=vitess
kubectl get events -n vitess --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "RFC: Atomic Distributed Transactions" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: This PR reject distributed transaction prepare on reserved connection. These are the connection that are using temporary tables or locking feature. They are not safe to allow support for atomic commit.
codeSnippets: Case 5: When Create Transaction Record fails. TM will roll back the transaction.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with vitess installed or the issue environment reproducible.
